compiled_state.title: Add PHASE_PLAN scratchpad counter and (Phase N of Y) annotations to onboarding SKILL.md
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
UPDATE

## Implementation Notes
Implement the scratchpad-based dynamic phase counter in `plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md`.

### 1. PHASE_PLAN initialization (Phase 1, Step 2: Initialize Scratchpad)

In Phase 1 Step 2 ("Initialize Scratchpad"), append PHASE_PLAN initialization after the scratchpad is created. The PHASE_PLAN must use a line-per-entry format (not a single comma-separated line) so sibling story 4c55-0c7a can prepend its dependency scan phase entry:

```bash
# Write PHASE_PLAN to scratchpad
echo "" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
echo "## PHASE_PLAN" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
echo "Phase 1: Auto-Detection" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
echo "Phase 1.5: Template Selection Gate" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
echo "Phase 1.6: Template Installation" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
echo "Phase 1.7: Post-Install Re-Detection" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
echo "Phase 2: Socratic Dialogue Loop" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
echo "Phase 3: Completion" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
```

### 2. Conditional PHASE_PLAN updates at skip points

At the Phase 1.5 "skip" branch (stack detected, not unknown — skip Phase 1.5/1.6/1.7 entirely), add instructions to remove those entries from PHASE_PLAN in the scratchpad.

At the Phase 1.7 success path (template installed → skip Phase 2), add instruction to remove "Phase 2" from PHASE_PLAN.

Use `sed -i` or equivalent to remove entries from the `## PHASE_PLAN` section in the scratchpad.

### 3. Phase counter display at each major phase heading

At each major phase heading (Phase 1, Phase 1.5, Phase 2, Phase 3), add an instruction block that computes and emits the phase counter:

```
At the start of this phase, read the PHASE_PLAN section from the scratchpad.
Compute: N = position of this phase in the list (1-indexed); Y = total entries.
Display to user: "(Phase N of Y)"
```

For Phase 3, Y should reflect only the phases that actually ran (PHASE_PLAN after all removals).

### 4. E2E coverage
E2E tests are not applicable — SKILL.md is a non-executable LLM instruction file. Behavioral correctness cannot be deterministically tested per Behavioral Testing Standard Rule 5. The structural boundary test (test_phase_counter_display, added in task 055c-8be7) is the appropriate coverage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tests pass (exit 0)
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] Lint passes (exit 0)
  Verify: ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py
- [ ] Format check passes (exit 0)
  Verify: ruff format --check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py
- [ ] Phase counter pattern present in SKILL.md
  Verify: grep -qE 'Phase [0-9]+ of [0-9]+' plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md
- [ ] PHASE_PLAN section present in SKILL.md
  Verify: grep -q 'PHASE_PLAN' plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md
- [ ] PHASE_PLAN appears in Phase 1 Step 2 (Initialize Scratchpad section area)
  Verify: [ $(grep -n 'PHASE_PLAN' plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md | head -1 | cut -d: -f1) -lt 130 ]
- [ ] test_phase_counter_display passes after this task
  Verify: bash tests/skills/test-onboarding-skill.sh 2>&1 | grep -q 'PASS.*test_phase_counter_display'
- [ ] Skill file is valid (not corrupted)
  Verify: test -f plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md && wc -l plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md | awk '{exit ($1 < 1260)}'
